data.title: Fintech startup Receive raises seed round, partners with Titanium Payments to launch Titanium Boost Business Mastercard for SMBs.
data.description: On July 17, 2025, in New York, fintech startup Receive secured a seed round of funding and collaborated with Titanium Payments to introduce the Titanium Boost Business Mastercard® aimed at granting small and medium-sized businesses real-time revenue access. This collaboration marks a significant step for Receive in democratizing financial services for SMBs.

data.title: 44% of working Americans view active social media as career risk; Boomers (48%) say it hurts, Gen Z (51%) see benefit.
data.description: A recent study conducted in the United States revealed contrasting views on the impact of social media presence on careers among different age groups. 48% of employed baby boomers expressed the belief that having an active social media presence can harm their career prospects. On the other hand, 51% of employed individuals from Generation Z believe that having a strong social media presence can actually benefit their careers. The study highlighted that 44% of working adults in the U.S. perceive an active social media presence as more detrimental than advantageous to one's career, whereas 37% hold the opposite view. These findings shed light on the evolving perceptions towards social media in the professional sphere.

data.title: Crypto hearings in Congress nearly derail as ties to Jeffrey Epstein scandal surface, per D.C. insiders.
data.description: The recent Crypto Week in Congress was marked by dramatic events on Wednesday, with implications linked to the Jeffrey Epstein scandal. Insights from policy insiders in D.C. suggest that the connection between them is more significant than anticipated.

data.title: Venture Global, Inc. announces Q2 2025 earnings release timing for a conference call in Arlington, Virginia.
data.description: Arlington, Virginia - Venture Global, Inc. has revealed the schedule for its second-quarter 2025 earnings release and associated conference call. The company will be announcing its financial results for that quarter during this period. The specific dates and details for the scheduled release and call have been made public by the company. Investors and stakeholders are encouraged to take note of this timing for the upcoming update on the company's performance during this specific quarter in 2025.

data.title: India's SMRs industry research predicts a $20 billion market opportunity by 2025, aiming to diversify energy sources.
data.description: A recent report entitled "Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) in India - Analyzing the $20 Billion Market Opportunity" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's collection. India is seeking to transition to a low-carbon and energy-secure future, which involves moving away from coal and incorporating clean baseload energy sources. By 2025, Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) have become a feasible and scalable solution to supplement existing large reactors and intermittent renewable energy sources, aligning with government policies. This report explores the potential for SMRs in India, highlighting a market opportunity valued at $20 billion.

data.title: Japan introduces first point-to-crypto program, enabling users to earn XRP, BTC, and ETH while spending yen.
data.description: A new point-to-crypto program has been introduced in Japan by Aplus and SBI VC Trade, marking the country's first such partnership. This initiative enables users to accrue XRP, BTC, and ETH through regular expenditures.

data.title: Listrak's Retail Outlook Reports for H2 2025 provide key trends and strategies for retailers, based on Listrak Intelligence.
data.description: Listrak, a marketing automation platform based in Lititz, Pennsylvania, has released Retail Outlook Reports for the second half of 2025. These reports, driven by Listrak Intelligence, highlight crucial trends and offer effective strategies to boost retail performance. The initiative aims to empower businesses with valuable insights. The reports come at a crucial time in the industry, as companies strategize for the latter part of the year.

data.title: Top altcoins surge with XRP and Dogecoin leading the gains, while 70+ of top 100 cryptos rise in 24 hours, excluding Bitcoin.
data.description: More than 70 out of the top 100 cryptocurrencies have experienced a rise in value in the last 24 hours. Surprisingly, Bitcoin is not among them.

data.title: NVIDIA improves GEMM kernel design with CUTLASS 3.x, enhancing code readability for Hopper and Blackwell with modular abstractions.
data.description: NVIDIA has rolled out CUTLASS 3.x, incorporating a modular, hierarchical approach to GEMM kernel design. This enhancement boosts code readability and expands compatibility to the latest architectures, such as Hopper and Blackwell. The update aims to enhance performance and efficiency, catering to a broader range of users.

data.title: Middle East Cybersecurity Market projected to reach USD 26.04 billion by 2030 with 9% CAGR. Investments, insurance demand, services, and startups driving growth.
data.description: Dublin - A recent report on the Middle East cybersecurity market predicts significant growth in the region. The market, currently valued at USD 16.75 billion in 2025, is expected to reach USD 26.04 billion by 2030, indicating a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 9%. The report highlights various solution types such as IAM, Firewall & VPN, Log Management & SIEM, and Antivirus/Antimalware, along with services like Professional (Risk & Threat Assessment, Training & Education) and Managed services. The cybersecurity market in the Middle East focuses on security types like Cloud and Application security.

data.title: Abbott Laboratories reports Q2 sales of $11.14 billion, beating consensus of $11.07 billion. Despite strong earnings, stock drops 10%.CEO Ford remains positive.
data.description: Abbott Laboratories (NYSE: ABT) exceeded expectations in the second quarter with reported sales of $11.14 billion, surpassing the consensus of $11.07 billion. Despite a 7.4% increase in sales on a reported basis (6.9% organically), the stock dropped nearly 10% as management revised down earnings and growth projections for the year. Adjusted earnings came in at $1.26, above the consensus of $1.25 and within the forecasted range of $1.23-$1.27. CEO Robert B. Ford highlighted achievements halfway through the year, including high single-digit organic sales growth, double-digit EPS growth, expanded margins, and progress in the new product pipeline. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Catalyst MedTech expands partnership with Arineta for sales in cardiac CT market, offering complete solution.
data.description: On July 17, 2025, Catalyst MedTech from Pittsburgh and Arineta from Caesarea, Israel, disclosed an extension of their strategic alliance in the field of diagnostic imaging solutions. Catalyst MedTech, a prominent player in this sector, strengthened its partnership with Arineta, known for its advancements in cardiovascular imaging solutions. This collaboration will encompass sales activities, enhancing their market presence and offerings.

data.title: K-Tech Solutions Co. Ltd. closes US$6.4M IPO with 1.6M Class A shares issued at US$4.00 per share. Headquartered in Hong Kong, the company provides toy product development solutions.
data.description: On July 17, 2025, K-Tech Solutions Company Limited, a Hong Kong-based toy products design house specializing in infant and pre-school educational toys, completed its initial public offering. The offering consisted of 1,600,000 Class A Shares priced at US$4.00 per share, generating total gross proceeds of US$6.4 million for the company. KMRK provides comprehensive product development services to toy brands, including design, prototype testing, production management, quality control, and after-sales support.

data.title: Snap-On Inc. reported Q2 2025 results with net sales of $1.179 billion and gross profit fell 0.30% YoY to $595.5 million.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, Snap-On Inc. (NYSE: SNA) exceeded expectations with their financial results. The quarterly net sales were reported at $1.179 billion, slightly up from the previous year. Despite a 0.7% organic decline in sales, a positive $8.6 million impact from favorable foreign currency translation helped boost the overall revenue above the estimated $1.16 billion. The gross margin experienced a slight contraction of 15 basis points to 50.5%, leading to a 0.30% decrease in gross profit, totaling $595.5 million. Financial services revenue saw an increase to $101.7 million, marking a 1.2% rise from the previous year, with operating earnings at $68.2 million. This was a decrease from $70.2 million reported in 2024. The consolidated operating earnings for the quarter amounted to $327.3 million, representing 25.5% of the total revenue generated.

data.title: SEALSQ successfully validates QS7001 Quantum Resistant Hardware Platform; chip to launch in Q4 2025 for first customers.
data.description: In Geneva, Switzerland on July 17, 2025, it was announced that a new chip featuring post-quantum security measures is projected to be released in the fourth quarter of 2025. This development is significant as it marks the integration of advanced security features into designs, potentially enhancing data protection for users. The chip is anticipated to cater to the needs of tech-savvy customers seeking robust security solutions in the rapidly evolving digital landscape.
